About the Position The Senior ROSA counsellor holds a case load of more complex ROSA clients and provides the TIPS service. The Senior ROSA counsellor also holds the additional role of providing a high level of support to the Manager in meeting service agreement, organisational goals and provision of mentorship to other team members in program around practice development, case conceptualisation and service delivery. To work collaboratively with the Program Manager and ROSA team towards best practice and high-quality therapeutic intervention for children and young people subject to statutory involvement and their care team. The Counselling and Intervention (ROSA) services are provided to children and young people under 18 years who are subject to statutory intervention and require support as a therapeutic response arising from a personal experience of physical, emotional or sexual abuse/harm and neglect. Counselling support is a targeted therapeutic response involving skilled and qualified practitioners listening attentively and patiently to the express feeling and circumstances of the child (and carer), perceiving difficulties from a child’s point of view, helping the child to see things differently and facilitating choice and positive change in the child’s life, conducted within a private and safe setting. The overall objective of counselling and intervention services is to increase the stability of care placements and support to families where ongoing intervention by the Department is required. A discreet component of Counselling and Intervention services is Outreach Placement Support. This activity is aligned to Counselling and Intervention in that it aims to achieve placement stability, but the placement may be at home following a period of residential care. This is referred to as TIPS within Centacare NQ. Key Responsibilities Include Provide support to children and young people with more complex and sensitive needs. Provide the TIPS service for the ROSA program through an attachment theory lenses. Complete assessment of child or young person’s needs through engagement and consultation with the family, the school/day care as needed, child safety and the carer. Complete the My Star outcome and develop a therapy plan for intervention based on assessment. Ensure consistent communication to Child Safety, maintaining reporting requirements as outlined in Information Provision to the Department of children, Youth Justice and Multicultural Affairs. Regular review of intervention plan goals through completion of subsequent outcome stars and progress reports and meetings with Child Safety as per procedure. Maintain clients’ files in accordance with program and agency procedures. Ensure working from a trauma informed and child development practice framework and utilizing evidenced based interventions. Complete regular safety assessments and discuss any safety concerns with line manager immediately as per policy. Participate and contribute to line management meetings, supervision, team meetings (which occur twice a fortnight) and staff days as required. Attend ROSA waitlist acceptance meetings with manager and engage in appropriateness of the referrals as they are received from the Department. Complete required administration tasks and ensure waitlist and csnet data is put in correctly. Demonstrate leadership by coaching, mentoring and motivating team members to achieve program goals; including assisting with induction of new staff and supporting staff in case conceptualisation and planning and completing sit in as required. Support and assist line manager as required and where appropriate and take initiative when opportunities exist. This includes around program development and business planning, team building, supporting staff at meetings as directed by Manager. To ensure job flexibility the role may be required to perform any other duties as nominated by Centacare North Queensland consistent with the classification descriptors detailed in the Social, Community, Home Care and Disability Services Industry Award 2010.
Job Role Skills and Experience: Qualifications & Experience The Position Requires Possession of a relevant degree in social work of psychology and a minimum of three years’ experience working therapeutically with children and young people who have experienced trauma. Demonstrated ability to impart knowledge and skills using a range of theoretical frameworks in a creative and flexible way regarding trauma informed practice. Understanding of core therapeutic framework and approaches. which underpin the program which are attachment theory, Developmental theory, trauma focussed CBT , Narrative Therapy and Expressive Therapy. Highly developed communication skills both written and verbal for working with a broad-based network of service providers and culturally diverse clientele. Knowledge of and understanding of the issues that impact on Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people in contemporary society. Demonstrated ability to communicate effectively and sensitively with Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people. An ability to understand and describe the impact of childhood trauma on behaviour, emotional regulation, attachment and approaches to support the child or young person and care team understand these impacts and therapeutic approaches to support Demonstrated ability to undertake risk assessments relating to suicidal ideation and child protection concerns and capacity to liaise with manage to ensure client safety. Demonstrated organizational, time management and administration skills. Sound understanding of program procedures and the child protection system. Demonstrated ability of Leadership, guidance support and guidance to staff independently. Current open drivers’ licence. Current, and ability to retain, both a Working with Children Blue Card check and Disability services Yellow Card. Desirable Knowledge skills and experience in one or more of the following – Group work, community education and training and Indigenous services. Relevant experience in Community Services and or within a Not-For-Profit or Faith Based sectors.

Incident rates and LTI reduction plans are implemented and achieved. The Port Store is a safe and healthy environment to work in through good housekeeping and timely identification and correction of all hygiene and safety issues and incidents. Relationship Management Takes responsibility for developing effective working relationships and liaison with all site team members to ensure a highly motivated, supportive and nurturing culture is adopted. Participate and support in the development of a committed culture of success and nurturing the growth and development of its people and resources. Effective working relationships exist with all site team members. Company pledge and values Understand and act in accordance with the company pledge and values. The Miraka pledge and values are understood and the required outcomes are achieved. Other Carry any other responsibilities as may be reasonably required and requested from time to time in the interests of the Company. The Company’s best interests are served at all times.

Job Role Description: The role is responsible for managing wage subsidy eligibility, negotiation, documentation, and reimbursement processes to ensure all eligible placements receive timely and compliant support. Engage with employers to confirm eligibility, explain subsidy options, and support them through the documentation process. Negotiate subsidy structure and agreements with employers to ensure mutually beneficial outcomes. Provide guidance to employers and participants on required evidence and timeframes. Monitor subsidy milestones, including scheduled evidence requests and payment points. Request documentary evidence from employers at each payment due date. Conduct thorough quality checks on all documentation submitted to ensure accuracy and compliance. Identify and escalate discrepancies, risks, or non-compliant documentation. Prepare and submit subsidy payments to Finance for processing. Ensure all reimbursements are accurately recorded in WFAO and ESS or other required systems. Process wage subsidies and other eligible items in compliance with regulatory guidelines. Maintain strong understanding of Outcome and Documentary Evidence Guidelines. Maintain accurate and compliant records for all claims, subsidy documents, and employer communication. Ensure all Post Placement Support (PPS) and outcome-related evidence is recorded correctly. Protect confidentiality of participant and employer information. Collaborate with internal teams to ensure alignment and accuracy in claims processing. Build strong employer relationships to support placements and communication. Provide clear communication regarding evidence requirements and payment timelines. Participate in audits and compliance reviews. Identify opportunities to streamline processes. Take corrective actions to address compliance or performance issues

About Training Alliance Group (TAG) Training Alliance Group (TAG) is a leading provider of employment and training services across Western Australia and a footprint in New South Wales. TAG helps individuals overcome barriers and achieve long-term employment. Partnering with Registered Training Organisations (RTOs), ABV Training & Consulting (ABV) and Mining, Transport & Construction Services (MTACS), TAG also delivers nationally recognised vocational education in industries like business, construction, and community services. With a strong focus on inclusion, quality, and real outcomes, TAG is committed to building futures through meaningful support, training, and job opportunities. Why Join TAG? When you join us, you’ll enjoy a range of benefits, including: Performance Incentives – Be rewarded for achieving great outcomes Employee Recognition Program – Celebrate milestones and success stories A well-structured fully supported induction program, with further development and training provided Refer-a-Friend Program – Earn rewards for helping us grow our team Employee Assistance Program (EAP) – Free, confidential wellbeing support Salary Sacrifice – Super – Maximise your superannuation benefits Professional Development Days – Dedicated time to focus on your growth Ongoing Learning & Development – Access training to build your skills and career Cash Out Annual Leave – Flexibility to suit your lifestyle and needs Annual Leave in Advance – Extra support when life requires it Make an Impact. Grow Your Career. Change Lives. Join a respected and expanding organisation where your work truly matters. At TAG, you’ll collaborate with supportive leaders, contribute to community-impacting initiatives, and thrive in a dynamic, purpose-driven environment. TAG is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We welcome applicants from all backgrounds, we welcome applications from Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people, applicants from culturally and linguistically diverse backgrounds, the LGBTIQ+ community and applicants who have a disability. If you need adjustments during the recruitment process, please let us know — we’re committed to a fair and inclusive experience for all. Join TAG and make a lasting difference—one career at a time. Job Role Title: Head of Strategy, Performance and Strategic Partnerships
Job Role Description: Role purpose The Head of Strategy, Performance and Strategic Partnerships is responsible for coordinating the development and communication of the enterprise strategy, monitoring the enterprise strategy implementation; major project co-ordination and establishing strategic partnerships. Key responsibilities Strategy • Lead the development, refinement, and coordination of AICD’s integrated strategy, ensuring alignment with the organisation’s long-term vision. • Conduct market and competitor analysis to identify trends and opportunities • Define key performance indicators (KPIs) to monitor enterprise strategy implementation and organisation performance. • Lead strategic planning sessions with senior leaders to set clear, actionable goals. • Offer data-driven insights and recommendations to guide decision-making. Governance & Performance • Develop performance dashboards and reporting mechanisms to measure progress. • Identify areas for improvement and shape initiatives to close performance gaps. • Ensure departmental objectives and initiatives are aligned with broader organisational strategy. • Support senior leaders with regular performance reports and insights that inform executive and board discussions. • As required, lead or assist with projects forming part of the enterprise strategy Strategic Partnerships • Identify and establish strategic partnerships with key stakeholders in prioritised sectors. • Collaborate with strategic partners to develop mutually beneficial initiatives. • Build awareness, understanding and alignment across internal functions to enable AICD to maximise the value of strategic partnerships. • Develop internal capability to initiate, develop and maintain strategic partnerships. Team Management • Plan, control and execute budgets where applicable using knowledge, skills, and resources to meet budget. • Lead, develop, motivate and coach employees to enhance their performance, capability and development. • Support employees in performance development including completing annual performance appraisals to achieve their career goals. • Manage poor performance in accordance with AICD’s policies and procedures. Ensure records of actions taken of any performance action are well documented. • Oversee regular reviews of systems/processes to implement continuous improvements. AICD Contribution • Contribute to the development of the department operation plan, suggesting ideas that create solutions for the team, and in turn the organisation. • Undertake or contribute to specific National and State projects or initiatives that support AICD’s strategic plan as required. • Proactively contribute feedback and ideas to AICD for continuous improvement and to achieve OKRs, budgets and strategic goals. Other duties in line with competencies and skills may be required on an ad hoc or permanent basis

Job Role Description: The Volunteer Coordinator at Company Name plays a crucial role in the management and support of volunteers, ensuring that all activities run smoothly and efficiently. This position involves a variety of responsibilities aimed at enhancing the volunteer experience and maximising their contributions to the organisation. Key Responsibilities: Recruit, train, and supervise volunteers, providing them with necessary orientation and resources. Develop and implement volunteer programmes that align with the organisation's goals and objectives. Maintain clear communication with volunteers, addressing queries and concerns in a timely manner. Organise volunteer recognition events to celebrate contributions and foster engagement within the team. Monitor volunteer activities to ensure compliance with policies, procedures, and safety regulations. Create schedules for volunteer shifts, ensuring adequate coverage for events and projects. Collaborate with other departments to identify opportunities for volunteer involvement in various initiatives. Prepare reports on volunteer engagement metrics to assess programme effectiveness and areas for improvement. Job Role Description: The Hospitality Manager is responsible for overseeing the operations of a hospitality establishment such as a hotel or restaurant. This includes managing staff members, ensuring customer satisfaction, and maintaining the financial health of the business. The Hospitality Manager will also be responsible for developing and implementing marketing strategies to attract new customers and retain existing customers.
Job Role Skills and Experience: Bachelors degree in Hospitality Management or related field. Extensive experience in hospitality operations and management. Strong leadership and team management skills. Excellent communication and customer service skills. Ability to develop and implement operational strategies and plans. Knowledge of industry regulations, policies, and procedures. Experience in budgeting, financial management, and cost control. Strong organizational and time-management skills. Ability to work in a fast-paced environment and handle multiple tasks. Experience in managing inventory, purchasing, and supply chain operations. Familiarity with hospitality technology and software. Flexibility to work evenings, weekends, and holidays.

Job Role Description: Primarily ensures the quality of locally manufactured products meets technical guidelines, drawings, and specifications. Secondarily ensures that incoming raw materials and finished goods adhere to company standards before integration. The nature and scope of the role is to work under limited supervision within routines, methods, and procedures where some discretion and judgment are required. Job Responsibilities: 1. Primary Focus: Production Quality Control & Compliance Data Metrics & Analytics: Captures data on failures and process performance, translating it into usable graphical information and test information reports to drive continuous improvement. Issue Logging & Facilitation: Logs quality issues and actively facilitates their progression, ensuring robust containment, proper disposition, thorough root-cause analysis, and final corrective actions. Production Sampling: Executes systematic production sampling inspections across manufacturing runs to verify ongoing quality consistency. ESD Compliance: Conducts regular Electrostatic Discharge (ESD) compliance verifications across production lines to safeguard sensitive components. Documentation Verification: Performs rigorous production documentation compliance verifications to ensure line logs, traveller sheets, and build records are accurately completed. Line Inspections: Performs Surface Mount Device (SMD) line inspections for reel placement and component selection for printed circuit board (PCB) production runs. Manufacturing Monitoring: Monitors ongoing locally and externally manufactured product quality based on defined technical guidelines and checklists developed in conjunction with Engineering. Cross-functional Liaison: Liaises actively with Engineering, Warehouse, and Production staff to facilitate production quality outcomes and corrective actions in a suitable timeframe. 2. Primary Focus: Defect Containment, Sorting & NCR Support Issue Logging & Facilitation: Logs production quality issues and actively facilitates their progression through the material management system. Containment & Disposition: Coordinates immediate quarantine and containment of non-conforming materials on the manufacturing floor to prevent further production escape. Sorting & Rework Support: Executes and oversees targeted sorting activities of in-process components and assemblies when defects are identified, collaborating on rework actions. Root-Cause Analysis: Participates in basic root-cause analysis and defect investigation alongside engineering teams. CAPA Progression: Facilitates the implementation and verification of immediate corrective actions on the line to ensure sustained resolution of process discrepancies. Priority Identification: Reviews the Incoming Material List and identifies priorities for incoming material inspection based on production needs. Order Review: Reviews incoming material details, such as purchase order numbers and quantities, to verify delivery accuracy. Technical Reference Gathering: Obtains relevant reference samples and engineering drawings for the purpose of inspecting raw materials and finished goods destined for Manufacturing and Wholesale. Dimensional & Finish Checks: Inspects incoming parts and performs critical dimension and finishes checks using precision instrumentation and measurement tools, referencing drawings, specifications, and AQL guidelines. Defect Reporting: Reports on problems identified with incoming material by generating Rejected Material Advice forms within the company’s material management system. 3. Secondary Focus: Inwards Goods & Incoming Inspection 4. General & Regulatory Compliance QA Support: Undertakes tasks related to quality matters and issues as required by the department manager in the evolution of the company. General: Executes any other duties as directed to support business objectives.
Job Role Skills and Experience: Experience: Technically skilled with experience in manufacturing and warehouse organisations, with a strong background working within established Quality Management Systems (QMS). Analytical Skills: Basic proficiency in data handling, defect logging, and presenting manufacturing metrics graphically. Attributes: Broadly "hands-on" with a proven ability to work independently under limited supervision as well as collaboratively within a cross-functional team. Communication: Computer literate with excellent written and verbal communication skills. Organisation: Well-organised with strong time management and prioritisation skills.
